140	lemma covariant_sequence_exact₁ {n₁ : ℕ} (x₁ : Ext X S.X₁ n₁)
141	    (hx₁ : x₁.comp (mk₀ S.f) (add_zero n₁) = 0) {n₀ : ℕ} (hn₀ : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
142	    ∃ (x₃ : Ext X S.X₃ n₀), x₃.comp hS.extClass hn₀ = x₁ := by
143	  have := covariant_sequence_exact₁' X hS n₀ n₁ hn₀
144	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff] at this
145	  exact this x₁ hx₁
146	
147	include hS in
148	lemma covariant_sequence_exact₂ {n : ℕ} (x₂ : Ext X S.X₂ n)
149	    (hx₂ : x₂.comp (mk₀ S.g) (add_zero n) = 0) :
150	    ∃ (x₁ : Ext X S.X₁ n), x₁.comp (mk₀ S.f) (add_zero n) = x₂ := by
151	  have := covariant_sequence_exact₂' X hS n
152	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff] at this
153	  exact this x₂ hx₂
154	
155	lemma covariant_sequence_exact₃ {n₀ : ℕ} (x₃ : Ext X S.X₃ n₀) {n₁ : ℕ} (hn₁ : n₀ + 1 = n₁)
156	    (hx₃ : x₃.comp hS.extClass hn₁ = 0) :
157	    ∃ (x₂ : Ext X S.X₂ n₀), x₂.comp (mk₀ S.g) (add_zero n₀) = x₃ := by
158	  have := covariant_sequence_exact₃' X hS n₀ n₁ hn₁
159	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff] at this
160	  exact this x₃ hx₃
161	
162	lemma postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono (L : C) {M N : C} (f : M ⟶ N) [hf : Mono f] :
163	    Function.Injective ((Ext.mk₀ f).postcomp L (add_zero 0)) := by
164	  rw [← AddMonoidHom.ker_eq_bot_iff, AddSubgroup.eq_bot_iff_forall]
165	  intro x hx
166	  obtain ⟨g, rfl⟩ := Ext.addEquiv₀.symm.surjective x
167	  simpa [← cancel_mono f] using hx
168	
169	lemma mono_postcomp_mk₀_of_mono (L : C) {M N : C} (f : M ⟶ N) [hf : Mono f] :
170	    Mono (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom <| (Ext.mk₀ f).postcomp L (add_zero 0)) :=
171	  (AddCommGrpCat.mono_iff_injective _).mpr (postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono L f)
172	
173	end CovariantSequence
174	
175	section ContravariantSequence
176	
177	variable {S : ShortComplex C} (hS : S.ShortExact) (Y : C)
178	
179	lemma singleFunctor_map_comp_hom [HasDerivedCategory.{w'} C]
180	    {X Y Z : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) {n : ℕ} (x : Ext Y Z n) :
181	    (DerivedCategory.singleFunctor C 0).map f ≫ x.hom =
182	      ((mk₀ f).comp x (zero_add n)).hom := by
183	  simp only [comp_hom, mk₀_hom, ShiftedHom.mk₀_comp]
184	
185	lemma preadditiveYoneda_homologySequenceδ_singleTriangle_apply
186	    [HasDerivedCategory.{w'} C] {Y : C} {n₀ : ℕ} (x : Ext S.X₁ Y n₀)
187	    {n₁ : ℕ} (h : 1 + n₀ = n₁) :
188	    (preadditiveYoneda.obj ((singleFunctor C 0).obj Y)).homologySequenceδ
189	      ((triangleOpEquivalence _).functor.obj (op hS.singleTriangle)) n₀ n₁ (by lia) x.hom =
190	      (hS.extClass.comp x h).hom := by
191	  rw [preadditiveYoneda_homologySequenceδ_apply,
192	    comp_hom, hS.extClass_hom, ShiftedHom.comp]
193	  rfl
194	
195	include hS in
196	/-- Alternative formulation of `contravariant_sequence_exact₂` -/
197	lemma contravariant_sequence_exact₂' (n : ℕ) :
198	    (ShortComplex.mk (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.g).precomp Y (zero_add n)))
199	      (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.f).precomp Y (zero_add n))) (by
200	        ext
201	        dsimp
202	        simp only [mk₀_comp_mk₀_assoc, ShortComplex.zero, mk₀_zero, zero_comp])).Exact := by
203	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
204	  have := (preadditiveYoneda.obj ((singleFunctor C 0).obj Y)).homologySequence_exact₂ _
205	    (op_distinguished _ hS.singleTriangle_distinguished) n
206	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at this ⊢
207	  apply Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact' (e₁ := Ext.homAddEquiv)
208	    (e₂ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (e₃ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (H := this)
209	  all_goals ext; apply singleFunctor_map_comp_hom (C := C)
210	
211	section
212	
213	variable (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : 1 + n₀ = n₁)
214	
215	/-- Alternative formulation of `contravariant_sequence_exact₁` -/
216	lemma contravariant_sequence_exact₁' :
217	    (ShortComplex.mk (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (((mk₀ S.f).precomp Y (zero_add n₀))))
218	      (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (hS.extClass.precomp Y h)) (by
219	        ext
220	        dsimp
221	        simp only [ShortComplex.ShortExact.extClass_comp_assoc])).Exact := by
222	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
223	  have := (preadditiveYoneda.obj ((singleFunctor C 0).obj Y)).homologySequence_exact₃ _
224	    (op_distinguished _ hS.singleTriangle_distinguished) n₀ n₁ (by lia)
225	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at this ⊢
226	  apply Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact' (e₁ := Ext.homAddEquiv)
227	    (e₂ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (e₃ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (H := this)
228	  · ext; apply singleFunctor_map_comp_hom (C := C)
229	  · ext; dsimp; apply preadditiveYoneda_homologySequenceδ_singleTriangle_apply
230	
231	/-- Alternative formulation of `contravariant_sequence_exact₃` -/
232	lemma contravariant_sequence_exact₃' :
233	    (ShortComplex.mk (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (hS.extClass.precomp Y h))
234	      (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (((mk₀ S.g).precomp Y (zero_add n₁)))) (by
235	        ext
236	        dsimp
237	        simp only [ShortComplex.ShortExact.comp_extClass_assoc])).Exact := by
238	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
239	  have := (preadditiveYoneda.obj ((singleFunctor C 0).obj Y)).homologySequence_exact₁ _
240	    (op_distinguished _ hS.singleTriangle_distinguished) n₀ n₁ (by lia)
241	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at this ⊢
242	  apply Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact' (e₁ := Ext.homAddEquiv)
243	    (e₂ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (e₃ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (H := this)
244	  · ext; dsimp; apply preadditiveYoneda_homologySequenceδ_singleTriangle_apply
245	  · ext; apply singleFunctor_map_comp_hom (C := C)
246	
247	open ComposableArrows
248	
249	/-- Given a short exact short complex `S` in an abelian category `C` and an object `Y : C`,
250	this is the long exact sequence
251	`Ext S.X₃ Y n₀ → Ext S.X₂ Y n₀ → Ext S.X₁ Y n₀ → Ext S.X₃ Y n₁ → Ext S.X₂ Y n₁ → Ext S.X₁ Y n₁`
252	when `1 + n₀ = n₁`. -/
253	noncomputable def contravariantSequence : ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat.{w} 5 :=
254	  mk₅ (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.g).precomp Y (zero_add n₀)))
255	    (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.f).precomp Y (zero_add n₀)))
256	    (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (hS.extClass.precomp Y h))
257	    (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.g).precomp Y (zero_add n₁)))
258	    (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.f).precomp Y (zero_add n₁)))
259	
260	lemma contravariantSequence_exact :
261	    (contravariantSequence hS Y n₀ n₁ h).Exact :=
262	  exact_of_δ₀ (contravariant_sequence_exact₂' hS Y n₀).exact_toComposableArrows
263	    (exact_of_δ₀ (contravariant_sequence_exact₁' hS Y n₀ n₁ h).exact_toComposableArrows
264	      (exact_of_δ₀ (contravariant_sequence_exact₃' hS Y n₀ n₁ h).exact_toComposableArrows
265	        (contravariant_sequence_exact₂' hS Y n₁).exact_toComposableArrows))
266	
267	end
268	
269	lemma contravariant_sequence_exact₁ {n₀ : ℕ} (x₁ : Ext S.X₁ Y n₀) {n₁ : ℕ} (hn₁ : 1 + n₀ = n₁)
270	    (hx₁ : hS.extClass.comp x₁ hn₁ = 0) :
271	    ∃ (x₂ : Ext S.X₂ Y n₀), (mk₀ S.f).comp x₂ (zero_add n₀) = x₁ := by
272	  have := contravariant_sequence_exact₁' hS Y n₀ n₁ hn₁
273	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff] at this
274	  exact this x₁ hx₁
275	
276	include hS in
277	lemma contravariant_sequence_exact₂ {n : ℕ} (x₂ : Ext S.X₂ Y n)
278	    (hx₂ : (mk₀ S.f).comp x₂ (zero_add n) = 0) :
279	    ∃ (x₁ : Ext S.X₃ Y n), (mk₀ S.g).comp x₁ (zero_add n) = x₂ := by
280	  have := contravariant_sequence_exact₂' hS Y n
281	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff] at this
282	  exact this x₂ hx₂
283	
284	lemma contravariant_sequence_exact₃ {n₁ : ℕ} (x₃ : Ext S.X₃ Y n₁)
285	    (hx₃ : (mk₀ S.g).comp x₃ (zero_add n₁) = 0) {n₀ : ℕ} (hn₀ : 1 + n₀ = n₁) :
286	    ∃ (x₁ : Ext S.X₁ Y n₀), hS.extClass.comp x₁ hn₀ = x₃ := by
287	  have := contravariant_sequence_exact₃' hS Y n₀ n₁ hn₀
288	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff] at this
289	  exact this x₃ hx₃
290	
291	lemma precomp_mk₀_injective_of_epi (L : C) {M N : C} (g : M ⟶ N) [hg : Epi g] :
292	    Function.Injective ((Ext.mk₀ g).precomp L (zero_add 0)) := by
293	  rw [← AddMonoidHom.ker_eq_bot_iff, AddSubgroup.eq_bot_iff_forall]
294	  intro x hx
295	  obtain ⟨f, rfl⟩ := Ext.addEquiv₀.symm.surjective x
296	  simpa [← cancel_epi g] using hx
297	
298	lemma mono_precomp_mk₀_of_epi (L : C) {M N : C} (g : M ⟶ N) [hg : Epi g] :
299	    Mono (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom <| (Ext.mk₀ g).precomp L (zero_add 0)) :=
300	  (AddCommGrpCat.mono_iff_injective _).mpr (precomp_mk₀_injective_of_epi L g)
301	
302	end ContravariantSequence
303	
304	end Ext
305	
306	end Abelian
307	
308	end CategoryTheory

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.ShortComplex.ModuleCat
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.QuotSMulTop
10	
11	/-!
12	# Categorical constructions for `IsSMulRegular`
13	-/
14	
15	@[expose] public section
16	
17	universe u v w
18	
19	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (M : ModuleCat.{v} R)
20	
21	open CategoryTheory Ideal Pointwise
22	
23	lemma LinearMap.exact_smul_id_smul_top_mkQ (M : Type v) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] (r : R) :
24	    Function.Exact (r • LinearMap.id : M →ₗ[R] M) (r • (⊤ : Submodule R M)).mkQ := by
25	  intro x
26	  simp [Submodule.mem_smul_pointwise_iff_exists,
27	    Submodule.mem_smul_pointwise_iff_exists]
28	
29	namespace ModuleCat
30	
31	/-- The short (exact) complex `M → M → M⧸xM` obtain from the scalar multiple of `x : R` on `M`. -/
32	@[simps]
33	def smulShortComplex (r : R) :
34	    ShortComplex (ModuleCat R) where
35	  X₁ := M
36	  X₂ := M
37	  X₃ := ModuleCat.of R (QuotSMulTop r M)
38	  f := ModuleCat.ofHom (r • LinearMap.id)
39	  g := ModuleCat.ofHom (r • (⊤ : Submodule R M)).mkQ
40	  zero := by
41	    ext x
42	    exact (LinearMap.exact_smul_id_smul_top_mkQ M r).apply_apply_eq_zero x
43	
44	lemma smulShortComplex_exact (r : R) : (smulShortComplex M r).Exact := by
45	  simp [smulShortComplex, ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact,
46	    LinearMap.exact_smul_id_smul_top_mkQ, -LinearMap.coe_smul]
47	
48	instance smulShortComplex_g_epi (r : R) : Epi (smulShortComplex M r).g := by
49	  simpa [smulShortComplex, ModuleCat.epi_iff_surjective] using Submodule.mkQ_surjective _
50	
51	end ModuleCat
52	
53	variable {M} in
54	lemma IsSMulRegular.smulShortComplex_shortExact {r : R} (reg : IsSMulRegular M r) :
55	    (ModuleCat.smulShortComplex M r).ShortExact where
56	  exact := ModuleCat.smulShortComplex_exact M r
57	  mono_f := by simpa [ModuleCat.smulShortComplex, ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective] using reg

1	/-

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Artinian.Module
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalRing.MaximalIdeal.Basic
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Nakayama
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.IsSMulRegular
12	
13	/-!
14	# Regular sequences and weakly regular sequences
15	
16	The notion of a regular sequence is fundamental in commutative algebra.
17	Properties of regular sequences encode information about singularities of a
18	ring and regularity of a sequence can be tested homologically.
19	However the notion of a regular sequence is only really sensible for Noetherian local rings.
20	
21	TODO: Koszul regular sequences, `H_1`-regular sequences, quasi-regular sequences, depth.
22	
23	## Tags
24	
25	module, regular element, regular sequence, commutative algebra
26	-/
27	
28	@[expose] public section
29	
30	universe u v
31	
32	open scoped Pointwise
33	
34	variable {R S M M₂ M₃ M₄ : Type*}
35	
36	namespace Ideal
37	
38	variable [Semiring R] [Semiring S]
39	
40	/-- The ideal generated by a list of elements. -/
41	abbrev ofList (rs : List R) := span { r | r ∈ rs }
42	
43	@[simp] lemma ofList_nil : (ofList [] : Ideal R) = ⊥ :=
44	  have : { r | r ∈ [] } = ∅ := Set.eq_empty_of_forall_notMem (fun _ => List.not_mem_nil)
45	  Eq.trans (congrArg span this) span_empty
46	
47	@[simp] lemma ofList_append (rs₁ rs₂ : List R) :
48	    ofList (rs₁ ++ rs₂) = ofList rs₁ ⊔ ofList rs₂ :=
49	  have : { r | r ∈ rs₁ ++ rs₂ } = _ := Set.ext (fun _ => List.mem_append)
50	  Eq.trans (congrArg span this) (span_union _ _)
51	
52	lemma ofList_singleton (r : R) : ofList [r] = span {r} :=
53	  congrArg span (Set.ext fun _ => List.mem_singleton)
54	
55	@[simp] lemma ofList_cons (r : R) (rs : List R) :
56	    ofList (r::rs) = span {r} ⊔ ofList rs :=
57	  Eq.trans (ofList_append [r] rs) (congrArg (· ⊔ _) (ofList_singleton r))
58	
59	@[simp] lemma map_ofList (f : R →+* S) (rs : List R) :
60	    map f (ofList rs) = ofList (rs.map f) :=
61	  Eq.trans (map_span f { r | r ∈ rs }) <| congrArg span <|
62	    Set.ext (fun _ => List.mem_map.symm)
63	
64	lemma ofList_cons_smul {R} [CommSemiring R] (r : R) (rs : List R) {M}
65	    [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] (N : Submodule R M) :
66	    ofList (r :: rs) • N = r • N ⊔ ofList rs • N := by
67	  rw [ofList_cons, Submodule.sup_smul, Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul]
68	
69	end Ideal
70	
71	namespace Submodule
72	
73	variable (M) [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup M₂]
74	    [Module R M] [Module R M₂] (r : R) (rs : List R)
75	
76	/-- The equivalence between M ⧸ (r₀, r₁, …, rₙ)M and (M ⧸ r₀M) ⧸ (r₁, …, rₙ) (M ⧸ r₀M). -/
77	def quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner :
78	    (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList (r :: rs) • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) ≃ₗ[R]
79	      QuotSMulTop r M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤ : Submodule R (QuotSMulTop r M)) :=
80	  quotEquivOfEq _ _ (Ideal.ofList_cons_smul r rs ⊤) ≪≫ₗ
81	    (quotientQuotientEquivQuotientSup (r • ⊤) (Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤)).symm ≪≫ₗ
82	      quotEquivOfEq _ _ (by rw [map_smul'', map_top, range_mkQ])
83	
84	/-- The equivalence between M ⧸ (r₀, r₁, …, rₙ)M and (M ⧸ (r₁, …, rₙ)) ⧸ r₀ (M ⧸ (r₁, …, rₙ)). -/
85	def quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopOuter :
86	    (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList (r :: rs) • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) ≃ₗ[R]
87	      QuotSMulTop r (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) :=
88	  quotEquivOfEq _ _ (Eq.trans (Ideal.ofList_cons_smul r rs ⊤) (sup_comm _ _)) ≪≫ₗ
89	    (quotientQuotientEquivQuotientSup (Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤) (r • ⊤)).symm ≪≫ₗ
90	      quotEquivOfEq _ _ (by rw [map_pointwise_smul, map_top, range_mkQ])
91	
92	variable {M}
93	
94	lemma quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner_naturality (f : M →ₗ[R] M₂) :
95	    (quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner M₂ r rs).toLinearMap ∘ₗ
96	        mapQ _ _ _ (smul_top_le_comap_smul_top (Ideal.ofList (r :: rs)) f) =
97	      mapQ _ _ _ (smul_top_le_comap_smul_top _ (QuotSMulTop.map r f)) ∘ₗ
98	        (quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner M r rs).toLinearMap :=
99	  quot_hom_ext _ _ _ fun _ => rfl
100	
101	lemma top_eq_ofList_cons_smul_iff :
102	    (⊤ : Submodule R M) = Ideal.ofList (r :: rs) • ⊤ ↔
103	      (⊤ : Submodule R (QuotSMulTop r M)) = Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤ := by
104	  conv => congr <;> rw [eq_comm, ← Quotient.subsingleton_iff]
105	  exact (quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner M r rs).toEquiv.subsingleton_congr
106	
107	end Submodule
108	
109	namespace RingTheory.Sequence
110	
111	open scoped TensorProduct List
112	open Function Submodule QuotSMulTop
113	
114	variable (S M)
115	
116	section Definitions
117	
118	/-
119	In theory, regularity of `rs : List α` on `M` makes sense as soon as
120	`[Monoid α]`, `[AddCommGroup M]`, and `[DistribMulAction α M]`.
121	Instead of `Ideal.ofList (rs.take i) • (⊤ : Submodule R M)` we use
122	`⨆ (j : Fin i), rs[j] • (⊤ : AddSubgroup M)`.
123	However it's not clear that this is a useful generalization.
124	If we add the assumption `[SMulCommClass α α M]` this is essentially the same
125	as focusing on the commutative ring case, by passing to the monoid ring
126	`ℤ[abelianization of α]`.
127	-/
128	variable [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
129	
130	open Ideal
131	
132	/-- A sequence `[r₁, …, rₙ]` is weakly regular on `M` iff `rᵢ` is regular on
133	`M⧸(r₁, …, rᵢ₋₁)M` for all `1 ≤ i ≤ n`. -/
134	@[mk_iff]
135	structure IsWeaklyRegular (rs : List R) : Prop where
136	  regular_mod_prev : ∀ i (h : i < rs.length),
137	    IsSMulRegular (M ⧸ (ofList (rs.take i) • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) rs[i]
138	
139	lemma isWeaklyRegular_iff_Fin (rs : List R) :
140	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs ↔ ∀ (i : Fin rs.length),
141	      IsSMulRegular (M ⧸ (ofList (rs.take i) • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) rs[i] :=
142	  Iff.trans (isWeaklyRegular_iff M rs) (Iff.symm Fin.forall_iff)
143	
144	/-- A weakly regular sequence `rs` on `M` is regular if also `M/rsM ≠ 0`. -/
145	@[mk_iff]
146	structure IsRegular (rs : List R) : Prop extends IsWeaklyRegular M rs where
147	  top_ne_smul : (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤
148	
149	end Definitions
150	
151	section Congr
152	
153	variable {S M} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup M₂]
154	    [Module R M] [Module S M₂]
155	    {σ : R →+* S} {σ' : S →+* R} [RingHomInvPair σ σ'] [RingHomInvPair σ' σ]
156	
157	open DistribMulAction AddSubgroup in
158	private lemma _root_.AddHom.map_smul_top_toAddSubgroup_of_surjective
159	    {f : M →+ M₂} {as : List R} {bs : List S} (hf : Function.Surjective f)
160	    (h : List.Forall₂ (fun r s => ∀ x, f (r • x) = s • f x) as bs) :
161	    (Ideal.ofList as • ⊤ : Submodule R M).toAddSubgroup.map f =
162	      (Ideal.ofList bs • ⊤ : Submodule S M₂).toAddSubgroup := by
163	  induction h with
164	  | nil =>
165	    convert AddSubgroup.map_bot f using 1 <;>
166	      rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, bot_smul, bot_toAddSubgroup]
167	  | @cons r s _ _ h _ ih =>
168	    conv => congr <;> rw [Ideal.ofList_cons, sup_smul, sup_toAddSubgroup,
169	      ideal_span_singleton_smul, pointwise_smul_toAddSubgroup,
170	      top_toAddSubgroup, AddSubgroup.pointwise_smul_def]
171	    apply DFunLike.ext (f.comp (toAddMonoidEnd R M r))
172	      ((toAddMonoidEnd S M₂ s).comp f) at h
173	    rw [AddSubgroup.map_sup, ih, map_map, h, ← map_map,
174	      map_top_of_surjective f hf]
175	
176	lemma _root_.AddEquiv.isWeaklyRegular_congr {e : M ≃+ M₂} {as bs}
177	    (h : List.Forall₂ (fun (r : R) (s : S) => ∀ x, e (r • x) = s • e x) as bs) :
178	    IsWeaklyRegular M as ↔ IsWeaklyRegular M₂ bs := by
179	  conv => congr <;> rw [isWeaklyRegular_iff_Fin]
180	  let e' i : (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList (as.take i) • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) ≃+
181	      M₂ ⧸ (Ideal.ofList (bs.take i) • ⊤ : Submodule S M₂) :=
182	    QuotientAddGroup.congr _ _ e <|
183	      AddHom.map_smul_top_toAddSubgroup_of_surjective e.surjective <|
184	        List.forall₂_take i h
185	  refine (finCongr h.length_eq).forall_congr @fun _ => (e' _).isSMulRegular_congr ?_
186	  exact (mkQ_surjective _).forall.mpr fun _ => congrArg (mkQ _) (h.get _ _ _)
187	
188	lemma _root_.LinearEquiv.isWeaklyRegular_congr' (e : M ≃ₛₗ[σ] M₂) (rs : List R) :
189	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs ↔ IsWeaklyRegular M₂ (rs.map σ) :=
190	  e.toAddEquiv.isWeaklyRegular_congr <| List.forall₂_map_right_iff.mpr <|
191	    List.forall₂_same.mpr fun r _ x => e.map_smul' r x
192	
193	lemma _root_.LinearEquiv.isWeaklyRegular_congr [Module R M₂] (e : M ≃ₗ[R] M₂) (rs : List R) :
194	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs ↔ IsWeaklyRegular M₂ rs :=
195	  Iff.trans (e.isWeaklyRegular_congr' rs) <| iff_of_eq <| congrArg _ rs.map_id
196	
197	lemma _root_.AddEquiv.isRegular_congr {e : M ≃+ M₂} {as bs}
198	    (h : List.Forall₂ (fun (r : R) (s : S) => ∀ x, e (r • x) = s • e x) as bs) :
199	    IsRegular M as ↔ IsRegular M₂ bs := by
200	  conv => congr <;> rw [isRegular_iff, ne_comm, ← Quotient.nontrivial_iff]
201	  let e' := QuotientAddGroup.congr _ _ e <|
202	    AddHom.map_smul_top_toAddSubgroup_of_surjective e.surjective h
203	  exact and_congr (e.isWeaklyRegular_congr h) e'.nontrivial_congr
204	
205	lemma _root_.LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr' (e : M ≃ₛₗ[σ] M₂) (rs : List R) :
206	    IsRegular M rs ↔ IsRegular M₂ (rs.map σ) :=
207	  e.toAddEquiv.isRegular_congr <| List.forall₂_map_right_iff.mpr <|
208	    List.forall₂_same.mpr fun r _ x => e.map_smul' r x

210	lemma _root_.LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr [Module R M₂] (e : M ≃ₗ[R] M₂) (rs : List R) :
211	    IsRegular M rs ↔ IsRegular M₂ rs :=
212	  Iff.trans (e.isRegular_congr' rs) <| iff_of_eq <| congrArg _ rs.map_id
213	
214	end Congr

216	lemma isWeaklyRegular_map_algebraMap_iff [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
217	    [Algebra R S] [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module S M]
218	    [IsScalarTower R S M] (rs : List R) :
219	    IsWeaklyRegular M (rs.map (algebraMap R S)) ↔ IsWeaklyRegular M rs :=
220	  (AddEquiv.refl M).isWeaklyRegular_congr <| List.forall₂_map_left_iff.mpr <|
221	    List.forall₂_same.mpr fun r _ => algebraMap_smul S r

223	variable [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup M₂] [AddCommGroup M₃]

227	lemma isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff (r : R) (rs : List R) :
228	    IsWeaklyRegular M (r :: rs) ↔
229	      IsSMulRegular M r ∧ IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs :=
230	  have := Eq.trans (congrArg (· • ⊤) Ideal.ofList_nil) (bot_smul ⊤)
231	  let e i := quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner M r (rs.take i)
232	  Iff.trans (isWeaklyRegular_iff_Fin _ _) <| Iff.trans Fin.forall_iff_succ <|
233	    and_congr ((quotEquivOfEqBot _ this).isSMulRegular_congr r) <|
234	      Iff.trans (forall_congr' fun i => (e i).isSMulRegular_congr (rs.get i))
235	        (isWeaklyRegular_iff_Fin _ _).symm

238	    IsWeaklyRegular M (r :: rs) ↔
239	      IsSMulRegular M r ∧
240	        IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
241	          (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) :=
242	  Iff.trans (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff M r rs) <| and_congr_right' <|
243	    Iff.symm <| isWeaklyRegular_map_algebraMap_iff (R ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) _ rs

257	  rw [isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff', ← restrictScalars_inj R (R ⧸ _),
258	    ← Ideal.map_ofList, ← Ideal.Quotient.algebraMap_eq, Ideal.smul_restrictScalars,

261	variable {M}
262	
263	namespace IsWeaklyRegular
264	
265	variable (R M) in
266	@[simp] lemma nil : IsWeaklyRegular M ([] : List R) :=
267	  .mk (False.elim <| Nat.not_lt_zero · ·)
268	
269	lemma cons {r : R} {rs : List R} (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r)
270	    (h2 : IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs) : IsWeaklyRegular M (r :: rs) :=
271	  (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff M r rs).mpr ⟨h1, h2⟩
272	
273	lemma cons' {r : R} {rs : List R} (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r)
274	    (h2 : IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
275	            (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r})))) :
276	    IsWeaklyRegular M (r :: rs) :=
277	  (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff' M r rs).mpr ⟨h1, h2⟩
278	
279	/-- Weakly regular sequences can be inductively characterized by:
280	* The empty sequence is weakly regular on any module.
281	* If `r` is regular on `M` and `rs` is a weakly regular sequence on `M⧸rM` then
282	  the sequence obtained from `rs` by prepending `r` is weakly regular on `M`.
283	
284	This is the induction principle produced by the inductive definition above.
285	The motive will usually be valued in `Prop`, but `Sort*` works too. -/
286	@[induction_eliminator]
287	def recIterModByRegular
288	    {motive : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) →
289	      IsWeaklyRegular M rs → Sort*}
290	    (nil : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → motive M [] (nil R M))
291	    (cons : {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) →
292	      (rs : List R) → (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r) →
293	      (h2 : IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs) →
294	      (ih : motive (QuotSMulTop r M) rs h2) → motive M (r :: rs) (cons h1 h2)) :
295	    {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → {rs : List R} →
296	    (h : IsWeaklyRegular M rs) → motive M rs h
297	  | M, _, _, [], _ => nil M
298	  | M, _, _, r :: rs, h =>
299	    let ⟨h1, h2⟩ := (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff M r rs).mp h
300	    cons r rs h1 h2 (recIterModByRegular nil cons h2)
301	
302	/-- A simplified version of `IsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegular` where the
303	motive is not allowed to depend on the proof of `IsWeaklyRegular`. -/
304	def ndrecIterModByRegular
305	    {motive : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → Sort*}
306	    (nil : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → motive M [])
307	    (cons : {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) →
308	      (rs : List R) → IsSMulRegular M r → IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs →
309	      motive (QuotSMulTop r M) rs → motive M (r :: rs))
310	    {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs} :
311	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs → motive M rs :=
312	  recIterModByRegular (motive := fun M _ _ rs _ => motive M rs) nil cons
313	
314	/-- An alternate induction principle from `IsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegular`
315	where we mod out by successive elements in both the module and the base ring.
316	This is useful for propagating certain properties of the initial `M`, e.g.
317	faithfulness or freeness, throughout the induction. -/
318	def recIterModByRegularWithRing
319	    {motive : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] →
320	      [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → IsWeaklyRegular M rs → Sort*}
321	    (nil : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] →
322	      [Module R M] → motive R M [] (nil R M))
323	    (cons : {R : Type u} → [CommRing R] → {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] →
324	      [Module R M] → (r : R) → (rs : List R) → (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r) →
325	      (h2 : IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
326	              (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r})))) →
327	      (ih : motive (R ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) (QuotSMulTop r M)
328	              (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) h2) →
329	            motive R M (r :: rs) (cons' h1 h2)) :
330	    {R : Type u} → [CommRing R] → {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] →
331	    [Module R M] → {rs : List R} → (h : IsWeaklyRegular M rs) → motive R M rs h
332	  | R, _, M, _, _, [], _ => nil R M
333	  | _, _, M, _, _, r :: rs, h =>
334	    let ⟨h1, h2⟩ := (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff' M r rs).mp h
335	    cons r rs h1 h2 (recIterModByRegularWithRing nil cons h2)
336	  termination_by _ _ _ _ _ rs => List.length rs
337	
338	/-- A simplified version of `IsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegularWithRing` where
339	the motive is not allowed to depend on the proof of `IsWeaklyRegular`. -/
340	def ndrecWithRing
341	    {motive : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) →
342	      [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → Sort*}
343	    (nil : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) →
344	      [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → motive R M [])
345	    (cons : {R : Type u} → [CommRing R] → {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] →
346	      [Module R M] → (r : R) → (rs : List R) → IsSMulRegular M r →
347	      IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
348	        (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) →
349	      motive (R ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) (QuotSMulTop r M)
350	        (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) → motive R M (r :: rs))
351	    {R} [CommRing R] {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs} :
352	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs → motive R M rs :=
353	  recIterModByRegularWithRing (motive := fun R _ M _ _ rs _ => motive R M rs)
354	    nil cons
355	
356	end IsWeaklyRegular

362	lemma isWeaklyRegular_singleton_iff (r : R) :
363	    IsWeaklyRegular M [r] ↔ IsSMulRegular M r :=
364	  Iff.trans (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff M r []) (and_iff_left (.nil R _))
365	
366	lemma isWeaklyRegular_append_iff (rs₁ rs₂ : List R) :
367	    IsWeaklyRegular M (rs₁ ++ rs₂) ↔
368	      IsWeaklyRegular M rs₁ ∧
369	        IsWeaklyRegular (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs₁ • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) rs₂ := by
370	  induction rs₁ generalizing M with
371	  | nil =>
372	    refine Iff.symm <| Iff.trans (and_iff_right (.nil R M)) ?_
373	    refine (quotEquivOfEqBot _ ?_).isWeaklyRegular_congr rs₂
374	    rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, bot_smul]
375	  | cons r rs₁ ih =>
376	    let e := quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner M r rs₁
377	    rw [List.cons_append, isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff, isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff,
378	      ih, ← and_assoc, ← e.isWeaklyRegular_congr rs₂]
379	
380	lemma isWeaklyRegular_append_iff' (rs₁ rs₂ : List R) :
381	    IsWeaklyRegular M (rs₁ ++ rs₂) ↔
382	      IsWeaklyRegular M rs₁ ∧
383	        IsWeaklyRegular (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs₁ • ⊤ : Submodule R M))
384	          (rs₂.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.ofList rs₁))) :=
385	  Iff.trans (isWeaklyRegular_append_iff M rs₁ rs₂) <| and_congr_right' <|
386	    Iff.symm <| isWeaklyRegular_map_algebraMap_iff (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs₁) _ rs₂
387	
388	end

393	lemma nil [Nontrivial M] : IsRegular M ([] : List R) where
394	  toIsWeaklyRegular := IsWeaklyRegular.nil R M
395	  top_ne_smul h := by
396	    rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, bot_smul, eq_comm, subsingleton_iff_bot_eq_top] at h
397	    exact not_subsingleton M ((Submodule.subsingleton_iff _).mp h)

399	lemma cons {r : R} {rs : List R} (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r)
400	    (h2 : IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs) : IsRegular M (r :: rs) :=
401	  (isRegular_cons_iff M r rs).mpr ⟨h1, h2⟩

403	lemma cons' {r : R} {rs : List R} (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r)
404	    (h2 : IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r})))) :
405	    IsRegular M (r :: rs) :=
406	  (isRegular_cons_iff' M r rs).mpr ⟨h1, h2⟩

408	/-- Regular sequences can be inductively characterized by:
409	* The empty sequence is regular on any nonzero module.
410	* If `r` is regular on `M` and `rs` is a regular sequence on `M⧸rM` then the
411	  sequence obtained from `rs` by prepending `r` is regular on `M`.
412	
413	This is the induction principle produced by the inductive definition above.
414	The motive will usually be valued in `Prop`, but `Sort*` works too. -/
415	@[induction_eliminator]
416	def recIterModByRegular
417	    {motive : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) →
418	      IsRegular M rs → Sort*}
419	    (nil : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → [Nontrivial M] →
420	      motive M [] (nil R M))
421	    (cons : {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) →
422	      (rs : List R) → (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r) → (h2 : IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs) →
423	      (ih : motive (QuotSMulTop r M) rs h2) → motive M (r :: rs) (cons h1 h2))
424	    {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs} (h : IsRegular M rs) : motive M rs h :=
425	  h.toIsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegular
426	    (motive := fun N _ _ rs' h' => ∀ h'', motive N rs' ⟨h', h''⟩)
427	    (fun N _ _ h' =>
428	      haveI := (nontrivial_iff R).mp (nontrivial_of_ne _ _ h'); nil N)
429	    (fun r rs' h1 h2 h3 h4 =>
430	      have ⟨h5, h6⟩ := (isRegular_cons_iff _ _ _).mp ⟨h2.cons h1, h4⟩
431	      cons r rs' h5 h6 (h3 h6.top_ne_smul))
432	    h.top_ne_smul
433	
434	/-- A simplified version of `IsRegular.recIterModByRegular` where the motive is
435	not allowed to depend on the proof of `IsRegular`. -/
436	def ndrecIterModByRegular
437	    {motive : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → Sort*}
438	    (nil : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → [Nontrivial M] → motive M [])
439	    (cons : {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) →
440	      (rs : List R) → IsSMulRegular M r → IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs →
441	      motive (QuotSMulTop r M) rs → motive M (r :: rs))
442	    {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs} : IsRegular M rs → motive M rs :=
443	  recIterModByRegular (motive := fun M _ _ rs _ => motive M rs) nil cons
444	
445	/-- An alternate induction principle from `IsRegular.recIterModByRegular` where
446	we mod out by successive elements in both the module and the base ring. This is
447	useful for propagating certain properties of the initial `M`, e.g. faithfulness
448	or freeness, throughout the induction. -/
449	def recIterModByRegularWithRing
450	    {motive : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] →
451	      [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → IsRegular M rs → Sort*}
452	    (nil : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] →
453	      [Module R M] → [Nontrivial M] → motive R M [] (nil R M))
454	    (cons : {R : Type u} → [CommRing R] → {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] →
455	      [Module R M] → (r : R) → (rs : List R) → (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r) →
456	      (h2 : IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
457	              (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r})))) →
458	      (ih : motive (R ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) (QuotSMulTop r M)
459	              (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) h2) →
460	            motive R M (r :: rs) (cons' h1 h2))
461	    {R} [CommRing R] {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs}
462	    (h : IsRegular M rs) : motive R M rs h :=
463	  h.toIsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegularWithRing
464	    (motive := fun R _ N _ _ rs' h' => ∀ h'', motive R N rs' ⟨h', h''⟩)
465	    (fun R _ N _ _ h' =>
466	      haveI := (nontrivial_iff R).mp (nontrivial_of_ne _ _ h'); nil R N)
467	    (fun r rs' h1 h2 h3 h4 =>
468	      have ⟨h5, h6⟩ := (isRegular_cons_iff' _ _ _).mp ⟨h2.cons' h1, h4⟩
469	      cons r rs' h5 h6 <| h3 h6.top_ne_smul)
470	    h.top_ne_smul
471	
472	/-- A simplified version of `IsRegular.recIterModByRegularWithRing` where the
473	motive is not allowed to depend on the proof of `IsRegular`. -/
474	def ndrecIterModByRegularWithRing
475	    {motive : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) →
476	      [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → Sort*}
477	    (nil : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) →
478	      [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → [Nontrivial M] → motive R M [])
479	    (cons : {R : Type u} → [CommRing R] → {M : Type v} →
480	      [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) → (rs : List R) →
481	      IsSMulRegular M r →
482	      IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
483	        (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) →
484	      motive (R ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) (QuotSMulTop r M)
485	        (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) →
486	      motive R M (r :: rs))
487	    {R} [CommRing R] {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs} :
488	    IsRegular M rs → motive R M rs :=
489	  recIterModByRegularWithRing (motive := fun R _ M _ _ rs _ => motive R M rs)
490	    nil cons

492	lemma quot_ofList_smul_nontrivial {rs : List R} (h : IsRegular M rs)
493	    (N : Submodule R M) : Nontrivial (M ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs • N) :=
494	  Submodule.Quotient.nontrivial_iff.mpr <|
495	    ne_top_of_le_ne_top h.top_ne_smul.symm (smul_mono_right _ le_top)
496	
497	lemma nontrivial {rs : List R} (h : IsRegular M rs) : Nontrivial M :=
498	  haveI := quot_ofList_smul_nontrivial h ⊤
499	  (mkQ_surjective (Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤ : Submodule R M)).nontrivial

593	    {rs'} (h'' : rs ~ rs') (h' : ∀ a b rs', (a :: b :: rs') <+~ rs →

608	    refine h.imp_right (ih (r :: rs₀) ?_ ?_) <;>
609	      exact List.perm_middle.subperm_right.mp ‹_›
610	  | swap a b t =>

613	    have : Ideal.ofList [b, a] = Ideal.ofList [a, b] :=
614	      congrArg Ideal.span <| Set.ext fun _ => (List.Perm.swap a b []).mem_iff

16	/-!
17	
18	# Hom(N,M) is subsingleton iff there exists a smul regular element of M in ann(N)
19	
20	Let `M` and `N` be `R`-modules. In this section we prove that `Hom(N,M)` is subsingleton iff
21	there exist `r : R`, such that `IsSMulRegular M r` and `r ∈ ann(N)`.
22	This is the case if `Depth[I](M) = 0`.
23	
24	## Main statements
25	
26	* `IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff` : for `R` module `N M`, `Hom(N, M) = 0`
27	  iff there is a `M`-regular in `Module.annihilator R N`.
28	
29	-/
30	
31	public section
32	
33	open IsLocalRing LinearMap Module
34	
35	namespace IsSMulRegular
36	
37	variable {R M N : Type*} [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R M] [Module R N]
38	
39	lemma linearMap_subsingleton_of_mem_annihilator {r : R} (reg : IsSMulRegular M r)
40	    (mem_ann : r ∈ Module.annihilator R N) : Subsingleton (N →ₗ[R] M) := by
41	  apply subsingleton_of_forall_eq 0 (fun f ↦ ext fun x ↦ ?_)
42	  have : r • (f x) = r • 0 := by
43	    rw [smul_zero, ← map_smul, Module.mem_annihilator.mp mem_ann x, map_zero]
44	  simpa using reg this
45	
46	lemma subsingleton_linearMap_iff [IsNoetherianRing R] [Module.Finite R M] [Module.Finite R N] :
47	    Subsingleton (N →ₗ[R] M) ↔ ∃ r ∈ Module.annihilator R N, IsSMulRegular M r := by
48	  refine ⟨fun hom0 ↦ ?_, fun ⟨r, mem_ann, reg⟩ ↦
49	    linearMap_subsingleton_of_mem_annihilator reg mem_ann⟩
50	  cases subsingleton_or_nontrivial M
51	  · exact ⟨0, ⟨Submodule.zero_mem (Module.annihilator R N), IsSMulRegular.zero⟩⟩
52	  · by_contra! h
53	    have hexist : ∃ p ∈ associatedPrimes R M, Module.annihilator R N ≤ p := by
54	      rcases associatedPrimes.nonempty R M with ⟨Ia, hIa⟩
55	      apply (Ideal.subset_union_prime_finite (associatedPrimes.finite R M) Ia Ia _).mp
56	      · rw [biUnion_associatedPrimes_eq_compl_regular R M]
57	        exact fun r hr ↦ h r hr
58	      · exact fun I hin _ _ ↦ IsAssociatedPrime.isPrime hin
59	    rcases hexist with ⟨p, pass, hp⟩
60	    let _ := pass.isPrime
61	    let p' : PrimeSpectrum R := ⟨p, pass.isPrime⟩
62	    have loc_ne_zero : p' ∈ Module.support R N := Module.mem_support_iff_of_finite.mpr hp
63	    rw [Module.mem_support_iff] at loc_ne_zero
64	    let Rₚ := Localization.AtPrime p
65	    let Nₚ := LocalizedModule.AtPrime p'.asIdeal N
66	    let Mₚ := LocalizedModule.AtPrime p'.asIdeal M
67	    let Nₚ' := Nₚ ⧸ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (Localization.AtPrime p)) • (⊤ : Submodule Rₚ Nₚ)
68	    have ntr : Nontrivial Nₚ' :=
69	      Submodule.Quotient.nontrivial_iff.mpr <| .symm <|
70	        Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator <|
71	          IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _
72	    let Mₚ' := Mₚ ⧸ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (Localization.AtPrime p)) • (⊤ : Submodule Rₚ Mₚ)
73	    let _ : Module p.ResidueField Nₚ' :=
74	      Module.instQuotientIdealSubmoduleHSMulTop Nₚ (maximalIdeal (Localization.AtPrime p))
75	    have := isAssociatedPrime_iff.mp <| AssociatedPrimes.mem_iff.mp
76	      (associatedPrimes.mem_associatedPrimes_atPrime_of_mem_associatedPrimes pass)
77	    rcases this.2 with ⟨x, hx⟩
78	    have : Nontrivial (Module.Dual p.ResidueField Nₚ') := by simpa using ntr
79	    rcases exists_ne (α := Module.Dual p.ResidueField Nₚ') 0 with ⟨g, hg⟩
80	    let to_res' : Nₚ' →ₗ[Rₚ] p.ResidueField := {
81	      __ := g
82	      map_smul' r x := by
83	        simp only [AddHom.toFun_eq_coe, coe_toAddHom, RingHom.id_apply]
84	        convert g.map_smul (Ideal.Quotient.mk _ r) x }
85	    let to_res : Nₚ →ₗ[Rₚ] p.ResidueField :=
86	      to_res'.comp ((maximalIdeal (Localization.AtPrime p)) • (⊤ : Submodule Rₚ Nₚ)).mkQ
87	    replace hx : maximalIdeal (Localization.AtPrime p) = (toSpanSingleton _ _ x).ker :=
88	      hx.trans (by simp [SetLike.ext_iff])
89	    let i : p.ResidueField →ₗ[Rₚ] Mₚ :=
90	      Submodule.liftQ _ (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton Rₚ Mₚ x) (le_of_eq hx)
91	    have inj1 : Function.Injective i :=
92	      LinearMap.ker_eq_bot.mp (Submodule.ker_liftQ_eq_bot _ _ _ (le_of_eq hx.symm))
93	    let f := i.comp to_res
94	    have f_ne0 : f ≠ 0 := by
95	      intro eq0
96	      absurd hg
97	      apply LinearMap.ext
98	      intro np'
99	      induction np' using Submodule.Quotient.induction_on with | _ np
100	      change to_res np = 0
101	      apply inj1
102	      change f np = _
103	      simp [eq0]
104	    absurd hom0
105	    let _ := Module.finitePresentation_of_finite R N
106	    contrapose f_ne0
107	    exact (Module.FinitePresentation.linearEquivMapExtendScalars
108	      p'.asIdeal.primeCompl).symm.map_eq_zero_iff.mp (Subsingleton.eq_zero _)
109	
110	end IsSMulRegular

      -- Then `(ih (M := MxM)).mpr (…)` ⇒ `(n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth …MxM`, which

-- iter-184 Lane G: closed via LES chase + sSup extraction + cons.
      --
      -- Step A: derive `∀ j < n, Ext^j(κ, MxM) = 0` from `hext` via the
      --   LES of `Ext^*(κ, ·)` on the SES `0 → M →[x] M → MxM → 0`.
      let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
        ModuleCat.smulShortComplex (ModuleCat.of R M) x
      have hS : S.ShortExact := hxReg.smulShortComplex_shortExact
      set κ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
        with hκ
      have hMxM_vanish : ∀ j < n, ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ
          (ModuleCat.of R MxM) j, e = 0 := by
        intro j hj e
        -- `e.comp hS.extClass rfl : Ext κ M (j+1) = 0` by `hext` at `j+1`.
        have he_ext : e.comp hS.extClass (rfl : j + 1 = j + 1) = 0 :=
          hext (j + 1) (by omega) _
        obtain ⟨x₂, hx₂⟩ :=
          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃ κ hS e rfl he_ext
        -- `x₂ : Ext κ M j = 0` by `hext` at `j`.
        have hx₂_zero : x₂ = 0 := hext j (by omega) _
        rw [hx₂_zero] at hx₂
        simpa using hx₂.symm
      -- Step B: apply `ih (M := MxM)` at index `n`.
      have hdepth_MxM : (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM :=
        (ih (M := MxM)).mpr hMxM_vanish
      -- Step C: unfold `depth MxM = sSup`-clause and extract a witness rs'
      --   of length ≥ n on MxM in 𝔪 (when n ≥ 1; the n = 0 case uses []).
      have hne_MxM : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R MxM) ≠ ⊤ :=
        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
      have hdepth_MxM_unfold :
          depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM
            = sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
              (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
              ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs } := by
        rw [depth, if_neg hne_MxM]
      obtain ⟨rs', hrs'_len_ge, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg⟩ :
          ∃ rs' : List R, n ≤ rs'.length ∧
            (∀ r ∈ rs', r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ∧
            RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs' := by
        rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with hn0 | hn_pos
        · subst hn0
          exact ⟨[], by simp, by simp,
            RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.nil R MxM⟩
        · have hlt : ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) <
              depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM := by
            have h1 : ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) < (n : ℕ∞) := by
              exact_mod_cast Nat.sub_lt hn_pos Nat.one_pos
            exact h1.trans_le hdepth_MxM
          rw [hdepth_MxM_unfold] at hlt
          obtain ⟨k, ⟨rs', hrs'_len_eq, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg⟩, hk_lt⟩ :=
            lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
          have hrs'_len_gt : n - 1 < rs'.length := by
            rw [← hrs'_len_eq] at hk_lt
            exact_mod_cast hk_lt
          refine ⟨rs', ?_, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg⟩
          omega
      -- Step D: truncate rs' to length n; resulting sequence is M-regular.
      let rs_n := rs'.take n
      have hrs_n_len : rs_n.length = n := by
        show (rs'.take n).length = n
        rw [List.length_take]
        omega
      have hrs_n_mem : ∀ r ∈ rs_n, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := fun r hr =>
        hrs'_mem r (List.mem_of_mem_take hr)
      have hrs_n_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs_n := by
        show RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM (rs'.take n)
        have hsplit : rs' = rs'.take n ++ rs'.drop n :=
          (List.take_append_drop _ _).symm
        have hwr : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM rs' :=
          hrs'_reg.toIsWeaklyRegular
        rw [hsplit] at hwr
        have hwr_n : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM (rs'.take n) :=
          ((RingTheory.Sequence.isWeaklyRegular_append_iff MxM _ _).mp hwr).1
        exact (IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal
          hrs_n_mem).mpr hwr_n
      -- Step E: cons x to form a length-(n+1) M-regular sequence in 𝔪.
      have hrs_M_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M (x :: rs_n) :=
        (RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_n).mpr ⟨hxReg, hrs_n_reg⟩
      have hrs_M_mem : ∀ r ∈ (x :: rs_n), r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
        intro r hr
        rcases List.mem_cons.mp hr with rfl | hr
        · exact hxMem
        · exact hrs_n_mem r hr
      have hrs_M_len : (x :: rs_n).length = n + 1 := by simp [hrs_n_len]
      -- Step F: conclude `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M` via `le_sSup`.
      have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ ⊤ :=
        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
      rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
      apply le_sSup
      refine ⟨x :: rs_n, ?_, hrs_M_mem, hrs_M_reg⟩
      exact_mod_cast hrs_M_len

      -- iter-184 Lane G: closed via Nakayama-driven `depth = sSup`
      -- extraction + cons-decomposition + LES chase using
      -- `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` + `covariant_sequence_exact₁`.
      intro _hdepth i _hi _e
      -- Step 1: unfold `depth M = sSup S_M` (Nakayama rules out `𝔪 • ⊤ = ⊤`).
      have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ ⊤ :=
        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
      have hdepth_M_unfold :
          depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
            = sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
              (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
              ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
        rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
      -- Step 2: extract a regular sequence rs of length > n in 𝔪 on M.
      have hlt : (n : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
        calc (n : ℕ∞)
            < ((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast Nat.lt_succ_self n
          _ ≤ _ := _hdepth
      rw [hdepth_M_unfold] at hlt
      obtain ⟨k, ⟨rs, hrs_len, hrs_mem, hrs_reg⟩, hk_lt⟩ := lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
      have hrs_len_gt : n < rs.length := by
        rw [← hrs_len] at hk_lt
        exact_mod_cast hk_lt
      -- Step 3: rs is non-empty (length > n ≥ 0); decompose rs = x :: rs_tail.
      rcases rs with _ | ⟨x, rs_tail⟩
      · -- impossible: empty list has length 0, not > n.
        simp at hrs_len_gt
      have hxMem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := hrs_mem x List.mem_cons_self
      have htail_mem : ∀ r ∈ rs_tail, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := fun r hr =>
        hrs_mem r (List.mem_cons_of_mem _ hr)
      have hcons := (RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_tail).mp hrs_reg
      have hxReg : IsSMulRegular M x := hcons.1

        hcons.2

      have hne_MxM : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R MxM) ≠ ⊤ :=

800	
801	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
802	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
803	```
804	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
805	```
806	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
807	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
808	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
809	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
810	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
811	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
812	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
813	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
814	
815	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/
816	
817	/-- **The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula.** Let `(R, 𝔪)` be a Noetherian local
818	ring and let `M` be a nonzero finite `R`-module of finite projective
819	dimension. Then
820	```
821	  pd_R(M) + depth_R(M) = depth(R).
822	```
823	
824	The hypothesis "finite projective dimension" is encoded by an explicit
825	upper bound `n : ℕ` on the projective dimension (so the formula compares
826	finite numeric quantities cleanly without `WithBot ℕ∞`-arithmetic
827	subtleties).
828	
829	iter-176+: the body is the induction on `depth(M)` outlined in the
830	chapter — base case via the minimal-finite-free-resolution +
831	"what is exact" + iterated `Module.depth_of_short_exact`; inductive step
832	via the snake lemma on multiplication by a common non-zero-divisor and
833	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`. For the iter-175
834	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
835	theorem auslander_buchsbaum_formula
836	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
837	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
838	    [Nontrivial M]
839	    (n : ℕ)
840	    (_hpd : _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R M = (n : WithBot ℕ∞)) :
841	    (n : ℕ∞) + Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
842	      = Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R := by
843	  sorry

36	open Abelian Limits ZeroObject
37	
38	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] [Abelian C]
39	
40	/-- An object `X` in an abelian category has projective dimension `< n` if
41	all `Ext X Y i` vanish when `n ≤ i`. See also `HasProjectiveDimensionLE`.
42	(Do not use the `subsingleton'` field directly. Use the constructor
43	`HasProjectiveDimensionLT.mk`, and the lemmas `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_iff` and
44	`Ext.eq_zero_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT`.) -/
45	class HasProjectiveDimensionLT (X : C) (n : ℕ) : Prop where mk' ::
46	  subsingleton' (i : ℕ) (hi : n ≤ i) ⦃Y : C⦄ :
47	    letI := HasExt.standard C
48	    Subsingleton (Ext.{max u v} X Y i)
49	
50	/-- An object `X` in an abelian category has projective dimension `≤ n` if
51	all `Ext X Y i` vanish when `n + 1 ≤ i` -/
52	abbrev HasProjectiveDimensionLE (X : C) (n : ℕ) : Prop :=
53	  HasProjectiveDimensionLT X (n + 1)
54	
55	namespace HasProjectiveDimensionLT
56	
57	variable [HasExt.{w} C] (X : C) (n : ℕ)
58	
59	lemma subsingleton [hX : HasProjectiveDimensionLT X n] (i : ℕ) (hi : n ≤ i) (Y : C) :
60	    Subsingleton (Ext.{w} X Y i) := by
61	  letI := HasExt.standard C
62	  have := hX.subsingleton' i hi
63	  exact Ext.chgUniv.{w, max u v}.symm.subsingleton
64	
65	variable {X n} in
66	lemma mk (hX : ∀ (i : ℕ) (_ : n ≤ i) ⦃Y : C⦄, ∀ (e : Ext X Y i), e = 0) :
67	    HasProjectiveDimensionLT X n where
68	  subsingleton' i hi Y := by
69	    have : Subsingleton (Ext X Y i) := ⟨fun e₁ e₂ ↦ by simp only [hX i hi]⟩
70	    letI := HasExt.standard C
71	    exact Ext.chgUniv.{max u v, w}.symm.subsingleton
72	
73	end HasProjectiveDimensionLT
74	
75	lemma Abelian.Ext.eq_zero_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT [HasExt.{w} C]
76	    {X Y : C} {i : ℕ} (e : Ext X Y i) (n : ℕ) [HasProjectiveDimensionLT X n]
77	    (hi : n ≤ i) : e = 0 :=
78	  (HasProjectiveDimensionLT.subsingleton X n i hi Y).elim _ _
79	
80	section
81	
82	variable (X : C) (n : ℕ)
83	
84	lemma hasProjectiveDimensionLT_iff [HasExt.{w} C] :
85	    HasProjectiveDimensionLT X n ↔
86	      ∀ (i : ℕ) (_ : n ≤ i) ⦃Y : C⦄, ∀ (e : Ext X Y i), e = 0 :=
87	  ⟨fun _ _ hi _ e ↦ e.eq_zero_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT n hi,
88	    HasProjectiveDimensionLT.mk⟩
89	
90	variable {X} in
91	lemma Limits.IsZero.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_zero (hX : IsZero X) :
92	    HasProjectiveDimensionLT X 0 := by
93	  letI := HasExt.standard C
94	  rw [hasProjectiveDimensionLT_iff]
95	  intro i hi Y e
96	  rw [← e.mk₀_id_comp, hX.eq_of_src (𝟙 X) 0, Ext.mk₀_zero, Ext.zero_comp]
97	
98	instance : HasProjectiveDimensionLT (0 : C) 0 :=
99	  (isZero_zero C).hasProjectiveDimensionLT_zero
100	
101	lemma isZero_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT_zero [HasProjectiveDimensionLT X 0] : IsZero X := by
102	  letI := HasExt.standard C
103	  rw [IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero]
104	  apply Ext.homEquiv₀.symm.injective
105	  simpa only [Ext.homEquiv₀_symm_apply, Ext.mk₀_zero]
106	    using Abelian.Ext.eq_zero_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT _ 0 (by rfl)
107	
108	lemma hasProjectiveDimensionLT_zero_iff_isZero : HasProjectiveDimensionLT X 0 ↔ IsZero X :=
109	  ⟨fun _ ↦ isZero_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT_zero X, fun h ↦ h.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_zero⟩
110	
111	lemma hasProjectiveDimensionLT_of_ge (m : ℕ) (h : n ≤ m)
112	    [HasProjectiveDimensionLT X n] :
113	    HasProjectiveDimensionLT X m := by
114	  letI := HasExt.standard C
115	  rw [hasProjectiveDimensionLT_iff]
116	  intro i hi Y e
117	  exact e.eq_zero_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT n (by lia)
118	
119	instance [HasProjectiveDimensionLT X n] (k : ℕ) :
120	    HasProjectiveDimensionLT X (n + k) :=
121	  hasProjectiveDimensionLT_of_ge X n (n + k) (by lia)
122	
123	instance [HasProjectiveDimensionLT X n] (k : ℕ) :
124	    HasProjectiveDimensionLT X (k + n) :=
125	  hasProjectiveDimensionLT_of_ge X n (k + n) (by lia)
126	
127	instance [HasProjectiveDimensionLT X n] :
128	    HasProjectiveDimensionLT X n.succ :=
129	  inferInstanceAs (HasProjectiveDimensionLT X (n + 1))
130	
131	instance [Projective X] : HasProjectiveDimensionLT X 1 := by
132	  letI := HasExt.standard C
133	  rw [hasProjectiveDimensionLT_iff]
134	  intro i hi Y e

# AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean

## Summary

**Lane G PRIMARY HARD BAR: HIT.** Both named residual sorries on
`depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` closed kernel-clean (Tier-1 axiom-clean,
only `propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound`).

**Sorry trajectory**: 3 → 2 (−1; loop file count).
**Declaration trajectory**: `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` was
sorry-laden (2 inline residuals) and is now sorry-FREE and kernel-clean.
This propagates: `depth_of_short_exact` (which transitively depends via
`ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth`) is also now kernel-clean.

**Helpers added**: 0 (existing helpers
`ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator`,
`ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth`,
`natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one` all reused as-is). Helper budget
unused.

**Secondary target `auslander_buchsbaum_formula`**: NOT attempted body
this iter. Realistic gap analysis below.

## depth_eq_smallest_ext_index — Inductive step (line 295)

### Attempt 1 (CLOSED, axiom-clean)

- **Approach**: shared LES-of-Ext-chase structure for both directions,
  using the SES `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0` built from
  `IsSMulRegular.smulShortComplex_shortExact`, plus the iter-183 axiom-clean
  helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` to kill the `x_*`-action
  on `Ext^*(κ, M)` whenever `x ∈ Ann(κ) = 𝔪`.
- **Result**: RESOLVED both directions.
- **Key insight**: `S.f = ofHom (x • LinearMap.id) = x • 𝟙_M` definitionally
  in ModuleCat (no `ofHom`-vs-`smul-id` defeq trap); then
  `_e.comp (mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = x • _e = 0` via
  `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_eq_comp_mk₀` + the killing helper.
  This unifies the LES chase for `i = 0` and `i ≥ 1` (the only branching
  is the `LES start (mono of S.f) vs `covariant_sequence_exact₁` API).

#### (⇒) Forward direction (line 366 sorry, now CLOSED)

**Steps:**
1. Unfold `depth M = sSup S_M` via `if_neg hne_M` (Nakayama:
   `𝔪 • ⊤ ≠ ⊤` for nontrivial finite M over local R, via
   `Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator`).
2. Extract regular sequence `rs` of length > n via `lt_sSup_iff`
   applied to `(n : ℕ∞) < ((n+1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M`.
3. Decompose `rs = x :: rs_tail`; cons-decompose via
   `RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff` gives `IsSMulRegular M x ∧
   IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x M) rs_tail`.
4. Build MxM, derive `depth MxM ≥ n` via `le_sSup` on the truncated
   `rs_tail.take n` (truncation regularity via
   `isWeaklyRegular_append_iff` ∘ `IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal`).
5. Apply `ih (M := MxM)` at index n to get
   `∀ j < n, Ext^j(κ, MxM) = 0`.
6. LES chase on the SES via `covariant_sequence_exact₁` for `i ≥ 1`
   and `postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono` for `i = 0`.

**Total LOC**: ~95 (the bulk is sSup extraction + truncation regularity;
the LES chase itself is ~15 LOC).

#### (⇐) Backward Step 3 (line 431 sorry, now CLOSED)

**Steps**: structurally analogous to forward direction but in reverse:
1. Derive `∀ j < n, Ext^j(κ, MxM) = 0` from `hext` via LES (use
   `covariant_sequence_exact₃` since we have `e.comp hS.extClass = 0`
   from `hext (j+1)`).
2. Apply `(ih (M := MxM)).mpr` to get `(n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth MxM`.
3. sSup extraction + truncation to length n on MxM.
4. Cons x to form `(x :: rs_n)` of length n+1 on M; apply `le_sSup`.

**Total LOC**: ~70.

### Tooling traps recorded

- **`set κ := … with hκ` renames pre-existing binders**: when the
  enclosing scope already has `_e : Ext (ModuleCat.of R (ResidueField R)) M i`,
  `set κ := ModuleCat.of R (ResidueField R)` rewrites occurrences in
  the goal/hypotheses and renames the dependent `_e` to `_e✝`. This
  caused a type mismatch (`hSf_kill : _e.comp … = 0` vs goal `_e✝ = 0`).
  **Workaround**: skip `set κ`, inline
  `(ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))` at the use sites.
  *(The existing `depth_of_short_exact` proof at L513 avoids this by doing
  `set κ` BEFORE any `intro` introduces an Ext-typed variable.)*
- **`map_zero`/`AddMonoidHom.map_zero` doesn't fire under `Ext.postcomp`
  abbreviation**: the postcomp is a `noncomputable abbrev`, and the
  unifier prefers leaving it folded. **Workaround**: explicit `change`
  to the `_e.comp (mk₀ S.f) (add_zero 0) =
  (0 : Ext …).comp (mk₀ S.f) (add_zero 0)` form, then `rw [Ext.zero_comp]`.
- **`mk₀_smul` rewrite needs `(R := R)` hint or different lemma**:
  `Ext.mk₀_smul (R := R)` is the existing pattern from
  `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator`. **Cleaner workaround used here**:
  use `(Ext.smul_eq_comp_mk₀ _e x).symm` directly, which packages
  `mk₀_smul + comp_smul + comp_mk₀_id` into a single
  `r • e = e.comp (mk₀ (r • 𝟙)) (add_zero _)` rewrite.
- **`show` vs `change` linter warning**: inside `have hrs_n_len : … = n
  := by show …; rw …` (where `rs_n` is a `let`-binding), the
  unfolding-via-`show` triggers the linter. Use `change` instead.

## auslander_buchsbaum_formula (line 835) — NOT ATTEMPTED THIS ITER

### Gap analysis

The plan agent estimated ~30-60 LOC via the "depth=smallest-Ext-index
reformulation + Stacks 00LF projDim equivalence" route. **This estimate
significantly underestimates the actual Mathlib substrate gaps.**

Stacks 090V (the AB proof) requires:

1. **Existence of a minimal finite free resolution** (Stacks
   `lemma-add-trivial-complex`). Mathlib `b80f227` has
   `Module.projectiveDimension` and `CategoryTheory.projectiveResolution`,
   but the *minimal*-resolution refinement (transitions in `𝔪 P_i`) is
   not standalone.
2. **"What is exact" criterion** (Stacks tag 00MF /
   `proposition-what-exact`): expresses exactness of a sequence of finite
   free modules in terms of depths of ideals of `r`-minors. **Not in
   Mathlib at the pinned commit.**
3. **Snake-lemma factorisation** for the SES
   `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0` to produce a minimal-resolution of `M/xM`
   over `R/xR` of the same length. **Non-trivial substrate work** —
   requires the snake lemma applied to a commutative diagram of
   resolutions.
4. **Depth-drops-by-one** (Stacks `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`):
   `depth (M/xM) = depth M - 1` for `x ∈ 𝔪` M-regular. **Not in
   Mathlib at the pinned commit**; the iter-184 closure of
   `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` makes the proof now accessible (via
   the LES of `Ext^*(κ, ·)` on the SES), but it would need its own
   helper lemma — outside this iter's helper budget for AB.

The directive's "depth=smallest-Ext-index reformulation" route
specifically asks whether AB can be proved using ONLY the now-closed
`depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` + `Module.projectiveDimension =
HasProjectiveDimensionLT (n+1) ↔ Ext^i(M, _) = 0 for i > n`. Even with
both Ext characterisations, the proof still needs to relate
`Ext^i(κ, R)` and `Ext^i(κ, M)` via the resolution — there is no
direct slick argument bypassing the minimal-resolution machinery.

### Recommendation for iter-185+

**Option A** (substrate-first, multi-iter): formalise minimal-finite-free-
resolution + "what is exact" + snake-lemma application as 3-4 separate
named lemmas, then assemble AB. Estimate: 4-8 iters.

**Option B** (skip AB, lean on Cohen–Macaulay corollary directly): note
that the file's downstream consumer (`CohenMacaulay.of_regular`) does
NOT actually use `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` — it uses the
regular-sequence-length-equals-Krull-dim argument directly. If A.4.a
truly only needs `CohenMacaulay.of_regular` and not the AB formula
itself, the AB theorem can be downgraded to a typed-sorry "wishlist"
declaration that need not block production. **Worth investigating** —
if true, iter-185 plan-phase can de-prioritise AB substantively and
focus the iter-185+ Lane G budget on `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal`
(the other remaining sorry, blocking `CohenMacaulay.of_regular`).

The chapter's `\section{Application: codim-1 extension…}` only references
Corollary `regular_cohen_macaulay`, suggesting A.4.a indeed does not
consume the AB formula directly. Recommend iter-185 reviewer audit to
confirm.

## exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal (line 944) — OFF-TARGET

Standing deferral. Blocked on Mathlib gap `IsRegularLocalRing → IsDomain`
(Stacks 00NQ; not in Mathlib at `b80f227`). The `lean_leansearch` for
"regular local ring is a domain" returns nothing relevant. Reachable
iter-185+ via either (a) formalising the Stacks 00NQ argument (no
embedded prime in (0) ⟹ (0) is prime), or (b) the alternative
`depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`-based proof of
`CohenMacaulay.of_regular` (now that the Ext characterisation is fully
proven, the "depth(R) ≥ d" lower bound might be obtainable via Koszul
homology of a system of parameters instead of an explicit regular
sequence).

## Axiom status

- `RingTheory.Module.depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`: **kernel-clean**
  (`propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound`).
- `RingTheory.Module.depth_of_short_exact`: **kernel-clean** (propagated
  via the now-axiom-clean `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`).
- `RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula`: still depends on `sorryAx`
  (body unchanged this iter).
- `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of_regular`: still depends on `sorryAx`
  via `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` (unchanged this iter).
- No new axioms introduced.

## Blueprint marker readiness

`depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` is now ready for `\leanok` on the *proof*
block (the statement was already `\leanok` per chapter
`Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex` L106). The `sync_leanok` phase between
prover and review will pick this up deterministically.

## Next-iter recommendations

1. **Confirm A.4.a's true dependency**: does A.4.a (the codim-1
   extension theorem) consume `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` directly,
   or only `CohenMacaulay.of_regular`? The chapter prose suggests only
   the latter. If confirmed, AB can be a low-priority wishlist
   declaration and `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` (the CM consumer)
   becomes the iter-185+ Lane G target.
2. **If AB is needed**: pursue Option A (substrate-first) starting with
   a `Module.minimal_free_resolution` def + existence lemma, gated on
   Mathlib's `ProjectiveResolution` infrastructure. ~4-8 iters.
3. **If only CM is needed**: pursue `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal`
   via either Stacks 00NQ (IsDomain extraction) or via a Koszul
   homology argument bypassing the explicit regular sequence. ~3-5
   iters.

## iter-184 · parallel prover

`set κ := … with hκ` (a common Lean 4 idiom in Mathlib's
`depth_of_short_exact`-style proofs) renames pre-existing binders whose
types contain the to-be-set expression, appending `✝`. Specifically,
when the enclosing scope already has `_e : Ext (ModuleCat.of R K) M i`
and you then `set κ := ModuleCat.of R K`, Lean renames the existing
`_e` to `_e✝` and uses `_e` for an unrelated fresh binder. This
produced cryptic "term has type `_e = 0` but expected `_e✝ = 0`" errors.

A search-tool surfaced hint or a lint warning ("`set` reshapes the
type of pre-existing dependent binder `_e`; consider doing `set` before
the `intro`") would have saved ~10 min here. Workaround = skip `set`
and inline at use sites, OR sequence `set` BEFORE any `intro` that
introduces a binder whose type uses the to-be-set expression.

EOF
